methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $150M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Einride
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Harbinger
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
ZeroAvia, a Series C-stage transportation company that has raised $150M+, operates in a sector with limited direct comparables. The Transportation peer universe includes 7 private companies (Einride, Harbinger, Rivian, The Boring Company, REGENT Craft among them) and no public traded comparables, constraining traditional benchmarking approaches.

Given the sparse peer set, Stage-Progression triangulation is the primary applicable method. Using total raised ($150M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical for Series C advancement yields $255M. This reflects the empirical step-up observed as companies progress from C-stage toward later funding rounds. The peer-median approach was inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum thresholds—only 2 same-stage Transportation peers were identified, below the three-datapoint minimum for statistical confidence.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $217M (low) to $293M (high), with $255M as the median point. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression output. Key uncertainties center on the representativeness of the 1.7x multiplier for ZeroAvia's specific capital efficiency and growth trajectory, and the absence of sector-specific public benchmarks limits external validation. Revenue scale, path to profitability, and competitive differentiation—not quantified in the available dataset—would materially inform placement within this range.
